arts.ttl: New contract renewals: Three Bayern Munich talents are here to stay for the long term
arts.sum: FC Bayern has signed three talented players from the campus for a longer period of time. Winger Binder impressed the coaches so much with the U-19s that he has been promoted to the U-23s, regular U-17 center back Pavlić stepped in for the U-19s in the both the league and the Youth League in an injury crisis and performed admirably, while midfielder Olychenko probably showed the greatest improvement of the lot, going from an afterthought for the U-19s to delivering genuinely eye-catching performances.

arts.ttl: Freiburg vs. Bayern Munich: Live stream, game time thread, how to watch
arts.sum: A look at the rosters and injuries for SC Freiburg and Bayern Munich. Be sure to stay tuned to Bavarian Podcast Works for all of your up to date coverage on Bayern Munich and Germany.

arts.ttl: Alphonso Davies to sign Bayern Munich contract extension until 2030
arts.sum: Alphonso Davies will sign a new contract extension at Bayern Munich next week, keeping him at the club until 2030, Christian Falk reports. The Canadian international once looked like he…

arts.ttl: “On the up” — Bayern Munich sporting director Christoph Freund offers encouragement to Leroy Sané
arts.sum: Bayern Munich winger Leroy Sané is playing for a new contract — and at times, it looks like it. “I firmly believe we’ll see good games from Leroy in the coming weeks.”

arts.ttl: Looking to the future? Christoph Freund explains Bayern Munich’s goalkeeper transfer links
arts.sum: Bayern Munich sporting director Christoph Freund explained the club’s extensive preparations at the goalkeeper position in this week’s press conference ahead of a match vs. That may be why Bayern looks set to sign Urbig immediately, even with solid coverage at the position for the rest of the year.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich bosses not worried over Harry Kane goal drought
arts.sum: Is Harry Kane starting to be a concern for Bayern Munich ? “When you have a young player, you might worry more, but Harry Kane has had so many highs and also some lows.

arts.ttl: 'Spectacular': Martin Keown says striker who left Man United for free was actually better than Harry Kane

Daily Schmankerl: Amid interest from Manchester City and Liverpool, Joshua Kimmich leaning toward Bayern Munich deal; Real Madrid to miss on Trent Alexander-Arnold this month; Benjamin Šeško staying at RB Leipzig; and MORE!
arts.sum: Despite that, Sky Sport journalist Kerry Hau says that Kimmich is still favoring a contract extension with Bayern Munich: Bayern Munich is still interested in Chelsea FC attacker Christopher Nkunku and could have less competition because Manchester United could be set to drop out of the race for the former RB Leipzig man:

arts.ttl: With heavy schedule, Bayern Munich’s Vincent Kompany can’t keep up with flailing Manchester City
arts.sum: Bayern Munich boss Vincent Kompany played at Manchester City for a long time and the club will clearly always hold a place in his heart. However, that does not mean that Kompany has spent much — if any — time following the recent collapse of Pep Guardiola’s side.

arts.ttl: Report: Bayern Munich won’t make big transfer splashes in January
arts.sum: Ousmane Diomande (around €60m price tag) and Rayan Cherki (€22.5m), although being watched, are not a topic for Bayern in the January transfer window . The Bavarians are balking at heavy price tags for reported transfer targets Ousmane Diomande and Rayan Cherki.
